zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Staunton於1266年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾內瓦可時，他的父親William de Staunton31歲，母親Isabel De Kirketon27歲。他於1296年與Elizabeth Joan De Breedon結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1341年在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾史道頓享年75歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾史道頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Staunton于1266年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾內瓦可时，他的父亲William de Staunton31岁，母亲Isabel De Kirketon27岁。他于1296年与Elizabeth Joan De Breedon结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1341年在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾史道頓享年75岁，埋葬在英国諾廷加姆爾史道頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Staunton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Caitlin, Ciaran, Cormac.

English and Irish: variant of Stanton . This English name has been established (in this spelling) since medieval times in Ireland (Mayo, Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as de Stonndún. Compare McEvilly .

variants.name: McEvilly
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Mhílidh ‘son of the knight’, from Gaelic míleadh ‘warrior, knight’. In County Mayo and County Cork, a family of English origin Gaelicized their name from Staunton to Mac an Mhílidh, apparently because of the latter's presumed similarity to the name of their ancestor, Milo de Stanton.

variants.name: Stanton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in England called Stanton or Staunton, especially Stanton in Staffordshire and Staunton in Worcestershire, all named with Old English stān ‘stone’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Most of the placenames come from their situation on stony ground, but in the case of Stanton Harcourt in Oxfordshire and Stanton Drew in Avon the reference is to the proximity of prehistoric stone monuments. The name has also sometimes been chosen by Ashkenazic Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames. This surname has also long been established in Ireland (especially Mayo).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Staunton was born in 1266, in Newark, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, William de Staunton, was 31 and his mother, Isabel De Kirketon, was 27. He married Elizabeth Joan De Breedon in 1296. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1341, in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75, and was buried in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Staunton est né en 1266, à Newark, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Staunton, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Isabel De Kirketon, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Joan De Breedon en 1296. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1341, à Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Stauntonが1266年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアニューアルクで生まれた時，父William de Stauntonは31歳，母Isabel De Kirketonは27歳であった。1296年にElizabeth Joan De Breedonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1341年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアスタウントンで75歳で死去し，イギリスノッティンガムシュアイアスタウントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Staunton(이)가 1266년 영국 노틴그함실르 뉴아르크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Staunton(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel De Kirketon(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1296년 Elizabeth Joan De Breedon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1341년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 스타운턴에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 스타운턴에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Joan De Breedon em 1296. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Staunton родился в 1266 г. в местности нюарк, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Staunton было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Isabel De Kirketon, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Joan De Breedon в 1296 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1341 г. в местности стаунтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности стаунтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия.

Se casó con Elizabeth Joan De Breedon en 1296. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1341, en Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.
